title: Get started with mcplite
text: mcplite yet CRAN. needed, install pak first, install development version GitHub:

title: Get started with mcplite
text: Save following server.R. defines one inventory tool starts server. tool combines R function client-facing name, description, argument schema. mcp_server() blocks R process serves requests. Put end client-launched script, tools defined sourced.
code: library(mcplite)  inventory_status <- tool(   function(item) {     stock <- c(apples = 12L, coffee = 0L, tea = 5L)      if (!item %in% names(stock)) {       stop("`item` must be apples, coffee, or tea.", call. = FALSE)     }      list(       item = item,       count = unname(stock[[item]])     )   },   name = "inventory_status",   description = "Report the local inventory count for a known item.",   arguments = list(     item = type_enum(       c("apples", "coffee", "tea"),       "Item whose inventory should be checked."     )   ) )  mcp_server(list(inventory_status))

title: Get started with mcplite
text: Add server entry MCP client’s configuration. Keep Rscript command, replace server path absolute path server.R file saved. script path must absolute client’s working directory guaranteed. Configuration file names locations vary client; use client’s MCP server configuration instructions.

what: 4. Restart the client and use the tool
title: Get started with mcplite
text: Restart client launches new server. client list inventory_status available tool. Ask check local inventory coffee. Success means result identifies coffee count 0; exact presentation varies client. client server communicate standard input output. server exits standard input closes.

title: Get started with mcplite
text: Ordinary R return values simplest default. Character values become text, JSON-serializable values, named list returned inventory_status, encoded JSON text content block. Use tool_result() tool needs explicit MCP content blocks, structured content, result metadata, error result. See reference pages tool(), tool_result(), content_*() type_*() helpers full API.

title: Get started with mcplite
text: Rich structured results: tool_result() supports explicit content structured output. output_schema advertises object-shaped structured content; content omitted, structured results include text fallback older clients. Protocol compatibility: mcplite negotiates supported MCP version client. content types structured output require newer MCP versions; reference documentation records compatibility details. ellmer: Compatible ellmer::tool() definitions can supplied directly mcp_server() interoperability needed. OpenTelemetry: Standard otel otelsdk configuration applies. Never send telemetry stdout: stdout reserved MCP protocol traffic. Use stderr remote exporter instead.

title: Get started with mcplite
text: Schemas describe inputs outputs clients; authorization sandboxing. tool function remains responsible domain validation, permission checks, credential handling, safe side effects, appropriate output payload limits. Run server operating-system data permissions tools need.

what: Lightweight stdio MCP Server for R
title: Lightweight stdio MCP Server for R
text: mcplite lets expose R functions Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools. runs small server standard input output, transport desktop MCP clients use launch local tool servers. Use want MCP client call R code data lookups, calculations, report helpers, local automation.

title: Lightweight stdio MCP Server for R
text: mcplite yet CRAN. Install GitHub:

title: Lightweight stdio MCP Server for R
text: Save complete server definition server.R: Configure MCP client launch script. command must resolve Rscript executable. Rscript discoverable, run Sys.("Rscript") R use returned path client’s command. Keep server script path absolute . Claude Desktop-style configuration looks like : Restart client check discovers add_numbers. first call, ask run add_numbers x = 2 y = 3; result 5. mcp_server() starts serving immediately runs standard input closes, launch MCP client rather interactive R console.
code: library(mcplite)  add_numbers <- tool(   function(x, y) {     x + y   },   name = "add_numbers",   description = "Add two numbers and return the result.",   arguments = list(     x = type_number("First number."),     y = type_number("Second number.")   ) )  mcp_server(list(add_numbers)) {   "mcpServers": {     "r-tools": {       "command": "Rscript",       "args": [         "--vanilla",         "/absolute/path/to/server.R"       ]     }   } }

title: Lightweight stdio MCP Server for R
text: tools, return ordinary R value. Character values become text, JSON-serializable values returned JSON text content block. Bare lists ordinary results, MCP result objects. Use tool_result() tool needs rich content blocks structured results. See ?tool_result result formats compatibility details.

title: Lightweight stdio MCP Server for R
text: mcplite supports local, client-launched stdio tool servers. implement HTTP transport MCP prompts, resources, sampling capabilities. Tools can still return supported content blocks; content blocks tool results, implementations MCP capabilities.

title: Lightweight stdio MCP Server for R
text: Compatible ellmer::tool() definitions can passed directly mcp_server(). ellmer optional needed tools defined mcplite.

text: See ?tool tool definitions, help("tool-types", package = "mcplite") schema helpers, ?tool_result help("content-blocks", package = "mcplite") rich results, ?mcp_server server behavior, scope, lifecycle, interoperability, tracing details.

what: Construct protocol-native MCP content blocks — content-blocks
title: Construct protocol-native MCP content blocks — content-blocks
text: constructors create content-block objects accepted tool_result(). R-facing names use snake_case; MCP names mimeType _meta created result serialized.

title: Construct protocol-native MCP content blocks — content-blocks
code: content_text(text, annotations = list(), meta = list())  content_image(data, mime_type, annotations = list(), meta = list())  content_audio(data, mime_type, annotations = list(), meta = list())  content_resource_link(   uri,   name,   title = NULL,   description = NULL,   mime_type = NULL,   size = NULL,   annotations = list(),   meta = list() )  content_resource(   uri,   text = NULL,   blob = NULL,   mime_type = NULL,   annotations = list(),   meta = list() )

title: Construct protocol-native MCP content blocks — content-blocks
text: text single text string. content_resource(), resource text payload; exactly one text blob must supplied. annotations Optional named MCP annotations. Known R-facing fields audience, priority, last_modified; named extension fields preserved serializable. meta Optional named outer content-block metadata. content_resource(), metadata duplicated inner resource contents object. data scalar base64 string containing inline image audio data. mime_type single MIME type string. Optional resources resource links. uri single non-empty author-supplied resource URI. name single non-empty resource-link name. title Optional resource-link display title. description Optional resource-link description. size Optional non-negative whole-number resource size bytes. blob scalar base64 string containing embedded resource payload; exactly one text blob must supplied.

title: Construct protocol-native MCP content blocks — content-blocks
text: validated content block use tool_result().

title: Construct protocol-native MCP content blocks — content-blocks
text: Image, audio, resource blob data must already scalar base64 strings. constructors read files, download URLs, infer MIME types, encode, decode, validate base64 data, invent resource URIs. Tool authors payload integrity, accurate MIME types, meaningful URIs, authorization, sanitization, payload size decisions. Resource links transport author-supplied URI. mcplite remains tools-server make URI readable implement resources/read. Embedded resources self-contained content blocks.

what: Run an MCP server for R tools over stdio — mcp_server
title: Run an MCP server for R tools over stdio — mcp_server
text: mcp_server() runs Model Context Protocol server standard input output. exposes tools created mcplite::tool() MCP clients can discover call R functions. Compatible ellmer::tool() objects can also supplied users already use ellmer, ellmer required ordinary mcplite tools.

title: Run an MCP server for R tools over stdio — mcp_server
Run an MCP server for R tools over stdio — mcp_server
text: tools Tool definitions expose. Supply one mcplite::tool() object list tool definitions. Compatible ellmer::tool() objects accepted supplied directly. instructions Optional server instructions advertise clients negotiate protocol version supports .

title: Run an MCP server for R tools over stdio — mcp_server
text: mcp_server() called side effect serving MCP requests. blocks current R process standard input closes.

title: Run an MCP server for R tools over stdio — mcp_server
text: mcplite supports lifecycle, ping, tools/list, tools/call subset protocol versions 2024-11-05, 2025-06-18, 2025-11-25. Tools may return ordinary R values legacy text conversion opt native content structured output tool_result(). Structured output, output schemas, audio, resource links, per-content metadata require MCP 2025-06-18 later. Text, images, embedded resources, annotations, result metadata also work 2024-11-05. server implement JSON-RPC batching, HTTP transports, sessions, prompts, resource listing reading, sampling, elicitation, roots, tasks, progress notifications, server-initiated requests. Embedded resource blocks self-contained, resource links make URIs readable mcplite. Supply tool definitions directly. client-launched workflows, put complete server setup script defines sources tools ends mcplite::mcp_server(actual_tool_or_list), launch script Rscript --vanilla /absolute/path//server.R.

title: Run an MCP server for R tools over stdio — mcp_server
text: mcplite automatically creates one OpenTelemetry server span every parsed MCP request notification passes JSON-RPC envelope validation. includes initialization notifications, ping, tool discovery calls, valid unknown methods. Blank input, JSON parse failures, malformed JSON-RPC envelopes create MCP operation spans. Tool authors need call otel::start_local_active_span() MCP operation tool invocation. server span remains active tool code runs, optional tool-authored spans can become children without required. mcplite create redundant automatic tool-execution child span. Provider exporter configuration belongs standard otel otelsdk environment variables APIs. mcplite add telemetry arguments, choose exporter, configure provider. exporter configured, tracing effective -op MCP behavior unchanged. safe stderr configuration OTEL_R_TRACES_EXPORTER=stderr Rscript --vanilla /absolute/path//server.R; remote exporter OTLP also suitable. use stdout console exporter stdio MCP server. Standard output reserved exclusively MCP protocol messages, telemetry written corrupt protocol stream. Remote W3C parent context may supplied params._meta.traceparent, optional params._meta.tracestate. Malformed propagation data ignored, _meta passed tool functions. default, spans contain selected low-cardinality operation metadata; raw requests responses, tool arguments results, _meta, trace headers, condition messages recorded span attributes.

title: Run an MCP server for R tools over stdio — mcp_server
code: if (identical(Sys.getenv("MCPLITE_CAN_BLOCK_PROCESS"), "true")) {   add_numbers <- tool(     function(x, y) {       x + y     },     name = "add_numbers",     description = "Add two numbers and return the result.",     arguments = list(       x = type_number("First number."),       y = type_number("Second number.")     )   )    mcp_server(list(add_numbers)) }

what: Define MCP tool argument schemas — tool-types

text: helpers create JSON Schema subset mcplite advertises tool arguments. describe inputs clients; tool functions still domain validation, coercion, authorization access checks, side-effect safety, output sanitization, rate limiting needed.

title: Define MCP tool argument schemas — tool-types
code: type_boolean(description = NULL, required = TRUE)  type_integer(description = NULL, required = TRUE)  type_number(description = NULL, required = TRUE)  type_string(description = NULL, required = TRUE)  type_enum(values, description = NULL, required = TRUE)  type_array(items, description = NULL, required = TRUE)  type_object(   .description = NULL,   ...,   .required = TRUE,   .additional_properties = FALSE )  type_from_schema(text = NULL, path = NULL)  type_ignore()

title: Define MCP tool argument schemas — tool-types
text: description Optional argument description. required Whether argument listed required parent schema. values Allowed enum values. items Type helper describing array item. .description Optional object description. ... Named properties object schemas. .required Whether object listed required parent schema. .additional_properties Whether allow additional properties. text JSON Schema list JSON string. path Path JSON Schema file. Exactly one text path must supplied.

title: Define MCP tool argument schemas — tool-types
text: lightweight mcplite tool type object.

title: Define MCP tool argument schemas — tool-types
text: Generated schemas MCP-compatible JSON Schema objects. $schema absent, MCP treats schemas JSON Schema 2020-12. type_from_schema() callers responsible supplying valid MCP-compatible schemas. List input preserves supplied R list shape: use named empty list {} unnamed empty list [].

title: Define MCP tool argument schemas — tool-types
code: type_string("A label.") #> $kind #> [1] "string" #>  #> $description #> [1] "A label." #>  #> $required #> [1] TRUE #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "mcplite_tool_type_string" "mcplite_tool_type"         type_array(type_integer(), description = "Integer values.") #> $kind #> [1] "array" #>  #> $description #> [1] "Integer values." #>  #> $required #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $items #> $kind #> [1] "integer" #>  #> $description #> NULL #>  #> $required #> [1] TRUE #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "mcplite_tool_type_integer" "mcplite_tool_type"         #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "mcplite_tool_type_array" "mcplite_tool_type"        type_object(   .description = "A labeled score.",   label = type_string(),   score = type_number(required = FALSE) ) #> $kind #> [1] "object" #>  #> $description #> [1] "A labeled score." #>  #> $required #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $properties #> $properties$label #> $kind #> [1] "string" #>  #> $description #> NULL #>  #> $required #> [1] TRUE #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "mcplite_tool_type_string" "mcplite_tool_type"        #>  #> $properties$score #> $kind #> [1] "number" #>  #> $description #> NULL #>  #> $required #> [1] FALSE #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "mcplite_tool_type_number" "mcplite_tool_type"        #>  #>  #> $additional_properties #> [1] FALSE #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "mcplite_tool_type_object" "mcplite_tool_type"         type_from_schema(list(   type = "string",   minLength = 1 )) #> $kind #> [1] "schema" #>  #> $description #> NULL #>  #> $required #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $schema #> $schema$type #> [1] "string" #>  #> $schema$minLength #> [1] 1 #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "mcplite_tool_type_schema" "mcplite_tool_type"

title: Define an MCP tool — tool
text: tool() wraps R function MCP metadata. resulting object can supplied directly, list, mcp_server(). Ordinary function return values use default single-text-block conversion; return tool_result() opt protocol-native content, structured output, result metadata.

title: Define an MCP tool — tool
code: tool(   fun,   description,   ...,   arguments = list(),   name = NULL,   annotations = list(),   output_schema = NULL )

title: Define an MCP tool — tool
text: fun Function expose tool. description single string describing use tool. ... used. Supply argument schemas arguments. arguments Named list argument schemas created type_*() helpers. name Optional tool name. omitted, tool() uses symbol name supplied fun; anonymous functions must supply name. Tool names must use 1 128 characters letters, digits, underscore, dot, hyphen. annotations Optional named list MCP tool annotations advertise. output_schema Optional object-shaped output schema created supported type_*() helper. Raw schema lists must wrapped type_from_schema(). schema advertised MCP 2025-06-18 later clients; tool authors remain responsible result conformance.

Pre-serialized JSON strings remain text, bare lists ordinary values, MCP result objects. output_schema advertises expected object-shaped structured_content MCP 2025-06-18 later clients. mcplite checks normalized schema JSON-object wire shape serializable, perform runtime schema validation.

what: Construct protocol-native MCP tool results — tool_result
title: Construct protocol-native MCP tool results — tool_result
text: tool_result() explicit opt-boundary returning protocol-native MCP results. content must created one content_*() constructors; ordinary R values, including bare lists JSON strings, retain legacy single-text-block conversion used tool().

title: Construct protocol-native MCP tool results — tool_result
code: tool_result(   content = list(),   structured_content = NULL,   is_error = FALSE,   meta = list() )

title: Construct protocol-native MCP tool results — tool_result
text: content One content block created content_*() constructor, ordered list blocks. Omitting content permits automatic text fallback generation structured_content; explicit content = list() suppresses fallback invalid. structured_content Optional named list representing JSON object. empty object allowed. mcplite validate value tool's advertised output schema. is_error Whether result reports tool error. meta Optional named result metadata. MCP wire names _meta created serialization.

title: Construct protocol-native MCP tool results — tool_result
text: protocol-native tool result return tool function.

title: Construct protocol-native MCP tool results — tool_result
text: structured_content supplied content omitted, tool_result() generates exactly one JSON text fallback block. Explicitly supplied content preserved -fallback appended. Structured content tool output schemas sent clients using MCP 2025-06-18 later; older clients receive generated text fallback.

title: Construct protocol-native MCP tool results — tool_result
code: tool_result(content_text("Done.")) #> $content #> $content[[1]] #> $type #> [1] "text" #>  #> $text #> [1] "Done." #>  #> $annotations #> named list() #>  #> $meta #> named list() #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "mcplite_content_text" "mcplite_content"      #>  #>  #> $structured_content #> NULL #>  #> $is_error #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $meta #> named list() #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "mcplite_tool_result"  tool_result(   content = list(     content_text("Generated the plot."),     content_image("base64-data", "image/png")   ) ) #> $content #> $content[[1]] #> $type #> [1] "text" #>  #> $text #> [1] "Generated the plot." #>  #> $annotations #> named list() #>  #> $meta #> named list() #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "mcplite_content_text" "mcplite_content"      #>  #> $content[[2]] #> $type #> [1] "image" #>  #> $data #> [1] "base64-data" #>  #> $mime_type #> [1] "image/png" #>  #> $annotations #> named list() #>  #> $meta #> named list() #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "mcplite_content_image" "mcplite_content"       #>  #>  #> $structured_content #> NULL #>  #> $is_error #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $meta #> named list() #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "mcplite_tool_result"  tool_result(structured_content = list(ok = TRUE, count = 2L)) #> $content #> $content[[1]] #> $type #> [1] "text" #>  #> $text #> {"ok":true,"count":2}  #>  #> $annotations #> named list() #>  #> $meta #> named list() #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "mcplite_content_text" "mcplite_content"      #>  #>  #> $structured_content #> $structured_content$ok #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $structured_content$count #> [1] 2 #>  #>  #> $is_error #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $meta #> named list() #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "mcplite_tool_result"

title: mcplite 0.1.0
text: First release lightweight, tools-Model Context Protocol (MCP) server standard input output (stdio), supporting MCP lifecycle, ping, tools/list, tools/call. Supports protocol versions 2024-11-05, 2025-06-18, 2025-11-25. Provides native tool JSON Schema helpers, optional interoperability compatible ellmer tool definitions. Adds explicit protocol-native tool results tool_result() content_text(), content_image(), content_audio(), content_resource_link(), content_resource() constructors. Results may contain ordered mixed content, structured content, annotations, metadata ordinary R return values retain existing text conversion. Adds object-shaped output_schema support tool(). Tool definitions results follow negotiated MCP version: structured output, output schemas, audio, resource links, content metadata available 2025-06-18 later, compatible fallback contained tool errors 2024-11-05. Handles protocol negotiation, request validation, JSON-RPC errors, MCP tool-call errors keeping stdio protocol stream usable. Automatically traces every envelope-valid MCP operation one OpenTelemetry server span, keeping tool-authored child spans optional. Standard otel otelsdk configuration owns provider exporter selection, remote W3C parent context accepted params._meta, request tool content captured default. Stdio servers must use stderr remote exporter stdout reserved MCP messages; exporter configured, tracing effective -op.
